factors.name: China Growth Data
factors.summary: China April retail sales collapsed to +0.2% YoY (exp 2.0%, prior 1.7%), worst since 2022; industrial production +4.1% (exp 5.9%). Dow has ~50% international revenue and heavy CAT/BA exposure to China demand.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike & Geopolitical Tension
factors.summary: WTI crude surged to 2-week highs (~$107) on Iran war deadlock; Trump issued fresh threats Sunday. Rising oil drives inflation fears and lifts long-end yields, pressuring dividend-heavy Dow names (utilities, REITs, consumer staples).

factors.name: Bond Selloff & Yield Surge
factors.summary: US 30-year yields hit 3-year highs; Japan 10Y hit 1996 high at 4.2%; global bond rout deepens on inflation fears per Bloomberg. Rate-sensitive Dow financials and dividend payers face headwinds; terminal rate expectations rising.

factors.name: Treasury Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Nominal yields soaring on oil-driven inflation expectations; real yields likely rising as well. Higher discount rates compress equity valuations, especially for lower-yielding industrial cyclicals in Dow portfolio.

factors.name: Dollar Strength vs Peers
factors.summary: USD rallying on hawkish Fed rate expectations and energy isolation (US net oil exporter status per RBC). However, multinational translation headwind for Dow's 50% foreign revenue exposure partially offsets.

factors.name: Industrial Metals & Copper Weakness
factors.summary: Copper extends retreat on China weakness and inflation fears per Bloomberg. Input cost pressures and demand signals deteriorating for CAT, MMM, HON—key Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: Global Risk-Off, Foreign Equities Selling
factors.summary: South Korea KOSPI saw $13.2B foreign investor selloff last week, volatility near record highs; Asia-Pacific FX weakening on Iran jitters. Contagion risk to US equities if geopolitical/growth fears persist.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Bias Emerging
factors.summary: Multiple strategists (Yardeni, others) flagging Fed must catch up with bond market hawkishness or lose control. Higher-for-longer rate expectations compress growth-oriented Dow names (tech, discretionary within index).

factors.name: Recent Price Action: -0.27% (4h), -0.30% (24h)
factors.summary: Dow down modestly; move is in early-to-mid phase, not yet extended. No capitulation or gap-down open yet, suggesting further downside possible if China/oil/yield fears deepen.

factors.name: Positive: China Trade Deals & Manufacturing Sentiment
factors.summary: White House announced $17B+ annual agricultural goods purchases from China; Singapore non-oil exports beat at +24.5% YoY. Partially offsets China demand weakness narrative.

factors.name: Samsung Labor Talks Resolution Hopes
factors.summary: Samsung shares jumped 5% on Seoul intervention in union dispute. Reduces near-term supply chain risk for semiconductor-exposed Dow names, but narrow positive.

screening.narrative: The Dow is under pressure from a convergence of macro headwinds: China's April economic data (retail sales weakest since 2022, industrial output missing badly) signals slowing global demand that hits Dow cyclicals (CAT, BA, MMM) directly. Concurrently, the Iran war deadlock is driving oil to 2-week highs and long-dated Treasury yields to 3+ year peaks, fueling inflation fears and hiking discount rates across dividend/stable-earner names. The global bond rout and foreign equity selloff (South Korea, Indonesia, Asia-Pacific FX) suggest contagion of risk sentiment. While the dollar is strengthening (a small plus for US corporates), the net effect is BEARISH for a Dow portfolio heavily exposed to China demand, energy input costs, and rate-sensitive valuations. The selloff is in early-mid phase (down ~0.3% overnight), suggesting further downside if geopolitical tensions or China growth concerns intensify ahead of US data this week.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting (date TBD, likely end-of-week) — potential hawkish surprise on inflation
ISM Manufacturing PMI (weekly/monthly, TBD) — direct read on Dow industrial health
Dow component earnings (NVIDIA, retailers, discretionary) — AI boom vs. consumer stress trade-off
US durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production — real-economy health check
Oil settlement & Iran ceasefire talks outcome — critical for inflation narrative reset
Japan GDP Q1, UK inflation, Canadian CPI — global rate expectations, carry unwinding
UST auction calendar stress — bonds struggling to clear
screening.risk_warning: If Iran war suddenly resolves or ceasefire announced → oil crashes, yields fall, and risk-on reversal could snap this bearish bias. Conversely, if China data deteriorates further (new stimulus fails) or oil spikes past $110 on Hormuz closure risk, downside could accelerate. Fed surprise dovish pivot (inflation print much cooler) would also flip narrative bullish.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (early selloff underway, -0.3% overnight). Confirm weakness with test of 49,200–49,300 support; if holds, consider short entry there targeting 48,800–49,000. If breaks below, watch for 48,500 next. Do not chase on open gap-downs; wait for stabilization / intra-day retest.
